Karlsruhe 3.7 MV VdG accelerator
4pi BaF2 calorimeter, which consists of 42
BaF2 crystals forming a closed shell with an inner
diameter of 20 cm and a thickness of 15 cm.
Thin 13C layers were produced by electron gun
evaporation of 99% isotopically enriched material.
The target backings consisted of water-cooled Cu
sheets, which were covered by 5 um thick Au layers to
minimize backgrounds from impurities in Cu.
The thickness of the 13C layer was 22 ug/cm2
Table III of Phys.Rev.C78(2008)025803
On. DATA multiplied by 1E+6.
the 10 MV Pelletron tandem accelerator.
During first campaign, the scattering cross section was
measured between 2.9 and 6.2 MeV.
Chamber contained 29 silicon detectors
13C targets 8 mm in diameter, which were mounted in
the center of a large scattering chamber. The
targets with a 13C enrichment of 99.9% were between
9.6 and 12.1 g/cm2 in thickness.
The total uncertainties of the data points
are dominated by the 10% systematic uncertainty of
the 13C thickness. Other systematic uncertainties,
i.e., from sputtering losses of the 13C target,
could be eliminated by the regular reference
measurements at 6.2 MeV. The duration of the
measurements was chosen such that the statistical
uncertainty of the detector with the lowest count
rate was always < 10%.
Data received from Michael Heil.
3.5 MV KN Van de Graaff accelerator
During second campaign, the scattering cross section
was measured between 2.5 and 3.5MeV.
Chamber contained 29 silicon detectors
13C targets 8 mm in diameter, which were mounted in
the center of a large scattering chamber. The
targets with a 13C enrichment of 99.9% were between
9.6 and 12.1 g/cm2 in thickness.
The total uncertainties of the data points
are dominated by the 10% systematic uncertainty of
the 13C thickness. Other systematic uncertainties,
i.e., from sputtering losses of the 13C target,
could be eliminated by the regular reference
measurements at 6.2 MeV. The duration of the
measurements was chosen such that the statistical
uncertainty of the detector with the lowest count
rate was always < 10%.
